objective: Data engineer with 8 years of experience designing and maintaining large-scale data pipelines, cloud infrastructure, and real-time analytics platforms. Seeking a principal engineering role where I can lead cross-functional teams in building reliable, cost-efficient data systems that drive strategic decision-making.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering
education.honors: Magna Cum Laude, Dean's List (6 semesters)

work_experience.title: Senior Data Engineer
description: Architected a real-time event streaming platform on Apache Kafka processing 2.4 million events per minute, reducing downstream latency by 68%.
Led a team of 5 engineers in migrating 140+ legacy ETL jobs from on-premise Informatica to Apache Airflow on AWS, completing the migration 3 weeks ahead of schedule.
Designed and implemented a medallion-architecture data lakehouse on Databricks and Delta Lake, consolidating 12 disparate data sources into a single unified analytics layer.
Established data quality monitoring using Great Expectations, catching schema drift and anomalies that previously went undetected for days.
Reduced monthly AWS spend by $18,000 through Spot Instance strategies, S3 lifecycle policies, and right-sizing Redshift clusters.

work_experience.title: Data Engineer II
description: Built HIPAA-compliant data pipelines ingesting electronic health records from 23 hospital systems, processing over 50 million patient encounters annually.
Developed a change-data-capture system using Debezium and Kafka Connect, enabling near-real-time synchronization between operational databases and the analytics warehouse.
Created dbt transformation models with full test coverage, reducing analyst-reported data discrepancies by 91%.
Partnered with the machine learning team to build feature engineering pipelines that served a patient readmission prediction model deployed across 8 hospital networks.
Authored internal documentation and runbooks that reduced on-call incident resolution time from 45 minutes to under 12 minutes on average.

work_experience.title: Junior Data Engineer
description: Maintained and optimized a suite of SQL Server Integration Services packages processing nightly batch loads of 200+ million transaction records.
Wrote Python and PySpark scripts to clean, deduplicate, and standardize customer data across three acquired subsidiary databases.
Assisted in the company's initial cloud migration to Azure, provisioning Data Factory pipelines, Blob Storage, and Synapse Analytics environments.
Built Tableau dashboards for the fraud analytics team, surfacing suspicious transaction patterns that contributed to a 14% increase in fraud detection rates.

awards.name: Engineering Excellence Award
awards.description: Recognized for leading the data lakehouse migration that unified the company's analytics platform and saved over $200,000 annually in infrastructure costs.

awards.name: Hackathon Winner – Best Data Solution
awards.description: Built a prototype real-time patient flow dashboard in 48 hours that was later adopted into the production monitoring suite.

awards.name: Outstanding Graduate Teaching Assistant
awards.issuer: University of Texas at Austin, Department of Computer Science
awards.description: Awarded for leading lab sections in the graduate-level Distributed Systems course with top student evaluation scores.

projects.name: OpenTransit Pipeline
projects.description: Open-source real-time ingestion framework for public transit GTFS-RT feeds. Streams bus and rail position data through Kafka into a PostGIS database, powering a live map dashboard used by three city transit advocacy groups.

projects.name: Cost Watcher
projects.description: Internal CLI tool that scans AWS accounts for idle or over-provisioned resources and generates Slack-delivered weekly savings reports. Adopted company-wide at Meridian Analytics after identifying $6,400/month in recoverable spend within its first week.

projects.name: MedSchema Validator
projects.description: Schema validation library for healthcare HL7 FHIR resources. Performs deep structural checks, terminology binding validation, and generates human-readable compliance reports. Used internally at Prism Health to vet incoming data from new hospital integrations.
